prompt: The stream is not closed after writing, causing incomplete data. What is the bug?
choices: ObjectOutputStream writes directly to disk without buffering
Without calling oos.close() or oos.flush(), buffered data may not be flushed to the file; the serialized object could be incomplete or missing
FileOutputStream automatically flushes when garbage collected
explanation: ObjectOutputStream buffers data internally. Without close() (which calls flush()) or explicit flush(), some bytes may remain in the buffer and not reach the file. Fix: use try-with-resources: try (ObjectOutputStream oos = new ObjectOutputStream(new FileOutputStream(...))) { oos.writeObject(...); }
